utility_evaluation.REASONING: First, count the number of items in the required format: 1) health_coverage, 2) property_coverage, 3) travel_coverage, 4) additional_services, 5) total_budget. That makes 5 total items. Next, check if all these items are present in the final coverage plan. health_coverage is present with physical and mental health insurance details and costs, plus an add-on and total monthly cost. property_coverage is present with plan, cost, and benefits. travel_coverage is given with plan, base cost, accident coverage, travel medical coverage, add-on, and total monthly cost. additional_services is present with liability plan name, cost, and coverage details. total_budget is present with monthly and annual breakdown. All items are fully specified with costs and relevant coverage details according to the format.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING:
Step 1: Identify all specific plan names and add-ons from the final coverage plan.
Health coverage physical plan is 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under primary_health_coverage.physical_health with rating 9.
Health coverage mental plan is also 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under primary_health_coverage.mental_health with rating 4.
Health coverage add-on is 'Preventive Care Package' under health_enhancement_services with rating 4.
Property coverage plan is 'InsurePlus Standard Coverage' under property_protection.home_content with rating 10.
Travel coverage plan is 'LifeFirst Standard Travel & Accident Plan' — this corresponds to 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' in mobility_coverage.travel_insurance with rating 9.
Travel coverage add-on is 'Premium Medical Network' under travel_enhancement_services with rating 7.
Additional services liability_plan is 'InsurePlus Professional & General Liability Bundle' — no direct rating is provided for liability plans in the ground-truth, so it will be omitted from the mapping.
Step 2: Map each identified plan/add-on to its rating from the ground-truth.
Step 3: Output JSON with exact names from ground-truth and ratings as numbers.
